channel.title: NPR Topics: Space
channel.description: NPR coverage of space exploration, space shuttle missions, news from NASA, private space exploration, satellite technology, and new discoveries in astronomy and astrophysics.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: NASA prepares to send four Artemis II astronauts on a lunar flyby
item.description: NASA is preparing to launch four people on a trip around the moon and back. It's the first time in more than a half-century humans are set to venture around the moon.
item.content:encoded: NASA is preparing to launch four people on a trip around the moon and back. It's the first time in more than a half-century humans are set to venture around the moon.
item.dc:creator: Nell Greenfieldboyce

item.title: Liftoff! NASA astronauts have launched on a historic journey to the moon
item.description: NASA's Artemis II mission lifted off from Kennedy Space Center in Florida at 6:35 p.m. Eastern Wednesday. The mission aims to send four astronauts around the moon on a roughly 10-day journey.
item.content:encoded:
NASA's Artemis II mission lifted off from Kennedy Space Center in Florida at 6:35 p.m. Eastern Wednesday. The mission aims to send four astronauts around the moon on a roughly 10-day journey.

(Image credit: Bill Ingalls)
item.dc:creator: Amina Khan

item.title: NASA readies for historic Artemis II moon launch
item.description: Artemis II, the first crewed mission to the Moon in more than 50 years, is set to launch as early as 6:24 p.m. Wednesday.
item.content:encoded: Artemis II, the first crewed mission to the Moon in more than 50 years, is set to launch as early as 6:24 p.m. Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Brendan Byrne

item.title: Retired NASA astronaut Leland Melvin on the goal of Artemis II and its significance
item.description: NPR's Leila Fadel talks to retired NASA astronaut Leland Melvin about the scientific and cultural significance of the Artemis II moon mission.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Leila Fadel talks to retired NASA astronaut Leland Melvin about the scientific and cultural significance of the Artemis II moon mission.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: NASA once touted the diversity of Artemis II's astronauts. Now? Not so much
item.description: The Artemis II mission crew contains four people -- including one woman and one Black man, both of whom will be the first on a lunar mission. But NASA hasn't been talking about these milestones much.
item.content:encoded: The Artemis II mission crew contains four people -- including one woman and one Black man, both of whom will be the first on a lunar mission. But NASA hasn't been talking about these milestones much.
item.dc:creator: Katia Riddle

item.title: A look back at how the Artemis II crew prepared for a historic moon mission
item.description: A four-astronaut crew is going on the first mission to send humans around the moon in more than 50 years. NPR's Scott Detrow visited with the crew while they were still training in Houston.
item.content:encoded: A four-astronaut crew is going on the first mission to send humans around the moon in more than 50 years. NPR's Scott Detrow visited with the crew while they were still training in Houston.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Brown

item.title: NASA is just days away from historic Artemis II moon launch
item.description: On Wednesday, the crew of NASA's Artemis II could blast off on a mission around the moon and back. No astronaut has ventured out to the moon since the 1970s.
item.content:encoded:
On Wednesday, the crew of NASA's Artemis II could blast off on a mission around the moon and back. No astronaut has ventured out to the moon since the 1970s.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Nell Greenfieldboyce

item.title: One idea to reduce data centers' load on the energy grid? Send them to space
item.description: In this roundup of space news, we talk about NASA's upcoming Artemis launch, space-based data centers and the new sci-fi movie Project Hail Mary.
item.content:encoded: In this roundup of space news, we talk about NASA's upcoming Artemis launch, space-based data centers and the new sci-fi movie Project Hail Mary.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Brumfiel

item.title: A scientist in Massachusetts launched the world's first modern rocket 100 years ago
item.description: One hundred years ago, Robert Goddard fired the first modern rocket. The launch, on what is now a golf course in Auburn, Massachusetts, paved the way for space exploration and much more.
item.content:encoded: One hundred years ago, Robert Goddard fired the first modern rocket. The launch, on what is now a golf course in Auburn, Massachusetts, paved the way for space exploration and much more.
item.dc:creator: Sam Turken

item.title: NASA targets Artemis II crewed moon mission for April 1 launch
item.description: A six-day launch window opens on April 1 from NASA's Kennedy Space Center in Florida. The lunar orbital mission would be the first time humans have returned to the moon since Apollo 17 in 1972.
item.content:encoded:
A six-day launch window opens on April 1 from NASA's Kennedy Space Center in Florida. The lunar orbital mission would be the first time humans have returned to the moon since Apollo 17 in 1972.

(Image credit: Gregg Newton)
item.dc:creator: Brendan Byrne
